text.title: Snapshots and Repository with Snapshot Support
text.summary: SnapshotStore implementation and EventSourcedOrderRepository that loads from snapshot + trailing events.

rule.statement: Take a snapshot every N events (50 is a common starting point). Load the latest snapshot, then replay only the events after its version.
rule.rationale: Without snapshots, replay time grows linearly with the number of events. Long-lived aggregates (thousands of events) cause unacceptably slow command handling.

rule.statement: Snapshots are an optimization, not the source of truth. The event log remains authoritative. If a snapshot is corrupt or missing, fall back to full replay.
rule.rationale: Relying on snapshots as the primary source of truth defeats the event-sourcing model and loses the audit trail.

example.code:
class SnapshotStore: def __init__(self, session): self._session = session
async def save(self, snapshot: Snapshot) -> None: await self._session.execute( text(""" INSERT INTO snapshots (stream_id, version, state, created_at) VALUES (:stream_id, :version, :state, :created_at) ON CONFLICT (stream_id) DO UPDATE SET version = :version, state = :state, created_at = :created_at """), {"stream_id": snapshot.stream_id, "version": snapshot.version, "state": json.dumps(snapshot.state), "created_at": snapshot.created_at}, )
async def get(self, stream_id: str) -> Optional[Snapshot]: result = await self._session.execute( text("SELECT * FROM snapshots WHERE stream_id = :s"), {"s": stream_id} ) row = result.fetchone() return Snapshot(row.stream_id, row.version, json.loads(row.state), row.created_at) if row else None

class EventSourcedOrderRepository: SNAPSHOT_FREQUENCY = 50
async def get(self, order_id: UUID) -> Optional[Order]: stream_id = f"order-{order_id}" snapshot = await self._snapshot_store.get(stream_id)
if snapshot: events = await self._event_store.read_stream(stream_id, from_version=snapshot.version) order = Order(**snapshot.state) order.version = snapshot.version else: events = await self._event_store.read_stream(stream_id) order = Order(id=order_id)
for event in events: order._apply(event, is_new=False) return order if order.version > 0 else None
async def save(self, order: Order) -> None: stream_id = f"order-{order.id}" events = order.collect_pending_events() if not events: return expected_version = order.version - len(events) await self._event_store.append(stream_id, events, expected_version) if order.version % self.SNAPSHOT_FREQUENCY == 0: await self._snapshot_store.save(Snapshot( stream_id=stream_id, version=order.version, state=self._serialize_order(order), created_at=datetime.utcnow(), ))
example.note: expected_version = current version minus pending count, because the pending events are not yet in the store. Snapshot taken after save at multiples of SNAPSHOT_FREQUENCY.
